title: hieracium murorum subsp. medianum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. medianum, also known as common hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, derived from its alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Hieracium murorum subsp. medianum can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
